data.body:
“Culturally, we have a tendency to flatten the ocean. To reduce it to a resource. A route. A problem to be managed. Pirate stories, almost accidentally, resist that flattening. They re-enchant the sea. They remind us that the ocean is not just a place we use—it is a place that changes us.”
#Pirates

data.body:
I worked the main stage at Boston Calling in 2019.

I remember Odesza playing and being completely floored. I was on "program duty" - easiest stressful job ever. You are the one who presses the "fallback" button for the live cameras out on the stage.

The cameras could not compete with the program.
